name: Cadre
description: Context-driven development methodology for organized, spec-first coding. JSON is the authoritative agent/runtime contract; Markdown exists only as platform shim or generated human projection.

triggers: user mentions specs, plans, tracks, or context-driven development
user asks about Cadre project status, track management, debugging, review, ship, land, release, archive, setup, or artifact sync
project is a polyrepo control repo with cadre/repos.json

startup.purpose: Verify Cadre MCP availability before every Cadre workflow

startup.rule: Every project-scoped Cadre MCP call must include a per-call root argument pointing at the project root or a path inside it
If Cadre MCP tools are unavailable, halt and ask the user to install, enable, or restart the Cadre plugin

lazyContext.detailMode: Use responseMode=detail only for full plans, code-intelligence evidence, or long style-guide content
rules: Do not read Markdown projections as canonical truth
Do not eagerly read the whole cadre/ tree on activation
Resolve live status from MCP packets and track metadata, not generated indexes

status.intent: Status, team board, or fleet board

operatingDefaults: Setup writes native Cadre event, message, formula, and local wisp state through packets
Shared sync is recommended for teams and packet-owned
Hosted provider evidence must come through official provider MCPs and be written back through Cadre packets
LSP/code-intelligence packets should be used for repo maps, impact checks, review assistance, diagnostics, test-impact hints, and bounded DAP debugging snapshots
forbidden: Do not mutate cadre/, provider state, indexes, worker state, event/message state, or merge/cleanup state outside Cadre packets
Do not use Markdown as alternate input, legacy import source, or runtime state
Do not manually edit generated Markdown projections as workflow state
